name: send_shard_empty_output
code:
proof fn phi_1_send_shard_empty_output(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, out: Set<Packet>,
    kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, recipient: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>,
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_shard(pre, post, out, kr, recipient, sm, true),
    ensures
        out == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If a shard with should_send=true produces empty output, recipients never receive delegated data, breaking liveness.

name: send_always_produces_message
code:
proof fn phi_2_send_always_produces_message(
    pre: SingleDelivery<Message>, post: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    m: Message, dst: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: Option<SingleMessage<Message>>, params: AbstractParameters,
)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, sm, params),
    ensures
        sm.is_Some(),
{
}
reason: If send_single_message always yields Some, the max_seqno overflow guard is vacuous and unbounded sends are possible.

name: missing_ack_nonzero
reason: A missing key returning nonzero acked count would fabricate acknowledgment history, corrupting retransmission logic.

name: reject_modifies_hashtable
code:
proof fn phi_4_reject_modifies_hashtable(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>,
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pkt.msg.arrow_Message_m() is Shard,
        next_shard_wrapper_must_reject(pre, pkt.msg.arrow_Message_m()),
        next_shard_wrapper(pre, post, pkt, out),
    ensures
        post.h != pre.h,
{
}
reason: If a rejected shard still mutates the hashtable, data is silently lost without any corresponding delegation transfer.

name: no_send_changes_hashtable
code:
proof fn phi_5_no_send_changes_hashtable(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, out: Set<Packet>,
    kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, recipient: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>,
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_shard(pre, post, out, kr, recipient, sm, false),
    ensures
        post.h != pre.h,
{
}
reason: If should_send=false still removes keys from h, data is destroyed without being delegated, causing permanent key loss.
